REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS MICHAEL SKAAR AND ROGER COLE'S MOTION TO STRIKE AND TAX COSTS IS DENIED. THE EARLY FILING OF DEFENDANT'S MEMORANDUM OF COSTS IS NOT A REASON TO STRIKE IT. (SEE PAMELA W. V. MILLSOM (1994) 25 CAL.APP.4TH 950, 961.) COSTS FOR PARTY DEPOSITIONS ARE EXPRESSLY RECOVERABLE BY STATUTE, AND THE COURT FINDS THE JAMS MEDIATION WAS ALSO REASONABLY NECESSARY TO THE CONDUCT OF THE LITIGATION AND THEREFORE PROPERLY RECOVERABLE AS A COST. MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Arthur Meirson, Esq. & A. Jeanne Grove, Esq. of GMH, (415-673-5600), for Michael J. Skaar, Plaintiff and moving party.
Laura S. Flynn, Esq. of Low Ball & Lynch, (415-981-6630), and Michael S. Burke, Esq. of Vogl & Meredith Burke LLP, (415-398-0200), for Thomas Clements Lacey, Defendant and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFs MICHAEL SKAAR and ROGER COLE's Motion To Strike And Tax Costs is DENIED. The early filing of Defendant's Memorandum of Costs is not a reason to strike it. (See Pamela W. v. Millsom (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 950, 961.) Costs for party depositions are expressly recoverable by statute, and the Court finds the JAMS mediation was also reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation and therefore properly recoverable as a cost. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not Reported, D501.
